name: Gravel Calculator
titleTag: Gravel Calculator - Estimate Gravel Needs | Smart Calculator Pro
metaDescription: Calculate the amount of gravel needed for your project. Estimate tons, cubic yards, and coverage for driveways and landscaping.
intro: Calculate how much gravel you need for your driveway, walkway, or landscaping project. Enter dimensions and depth to get material estimates.
shortDescription: Calculate gravel volume and weight needed.
longDescription: This calculator estimates the amount of gravel needed based on area dimensions and desired depth. Results include cubic yards, tons, and coverage area.
secondaryKeywords: how much gravel do i need
problemSolved: Helps users estimate gravel materials for landscaping and construction.

inputs.label: Area Type
inputs.explanation: Select how you're measuring the area.

inputs.label: Length
inputs.explanation: The length of the area in feet.

inputs.label: Width
inputs.explanation: The width of the area in feet.

inputs.label: Depth
inputs.explanation: The depth of gravel in inches.

inputs.label: Gravel Type
inputs.explanation: Different gravel types have different densities.

formula.label: Gravel Volume Formula
formula.plainText: Volume in cubic yards equals length times width times depth (in feet), divided by 27.

formulaExplanation: Convert depth from inches to feet by dividing by 12
Calculate area in square feet: length × width
Calculate volume in cubic yards: area × depth (in feet) ÷ 27
methodologyNotes: One cubic yard covers approximately 108 square feet at 3 inches deep
Standard gravel weighs approximately 1.4 tons per cubic yard
Different gravel types have different densities
Always order 10-15% extra for compaction and settling

example.scenario: A rectangular driveway 10 feet by 20 feet, 2 inches deep.
example.resultSummary: You'll need approximately 1.23 cubic yards of gravel, weighing about 1.72 tons, covering 200 square feet.

resultInterpretation.defaultText: Use these estimates to order gravel. Gravel settles and compacts over time, so consider ordering slightly more than calculated.
warningMessages: Order 10-15% extra to account for compaction and settling.
Gravel depth may need to be deeper for driveways to support vehicle weight.
Consider using a landscape fabric underneath to prevent weed growth.

faq.question: How much gravel do I need?
faq.answer: Multiply the area in square feet by the desired depth in feet, then divide by 27 to get cubic yards. Order 10-15% extra for compaction.

faq.question: How deep should gravel be?
faq.answer: For walkways and patios, 2-3 inches is usually sufficient. For driveways, use 4-6 inches or more to support vehicle weight.

faq.question: How much does a cubic yard of gravel weigh?
faq.answer: A cubic yard of standard gravel typically weighs about 1.4 tons (2,800 lbs). This can vary depending on the type of gravel and moisture content.

disclaimer: This calculator provides estimates only. Actual material needs may vary based on site conditions, gravel type, and compaction.
